records.ety: [L. integratus, p. p. of integrare to make whole, renew: cf. F. intgrer. See Integer, Entire.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Integrated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Integrating (?).]

defs: To form into one whole; to make entire; to complete; to renew; to restore; to perfect.
quotes: Two distinct substances, the soul and body, go to compound and integrate the man.

defs: To indicate the whole of; to give the sum or total of; as, an integrating anemometer, one that indicates or registers the entire action of the wind in a given time.
To subject to the operation of integration; to find the integral of.
